title: Growth Rate and Terminal Value: The terminal value in DCF analysis represents the cash flows...
summary: Assess the role of terminal value in DCF analysis as capturing value beyond the forecast period.
prompt: The terminal value in DCF analysis represents the cash flows beyond the projection period.
solution: The terminal value in a DCF analysis is a critical component that estimates the value of a business at the end of the explicit forecast period, accounting for all future cash flows beyond that point.
